title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Work Item
summary: Plan the trace-safe widening from the current first-successor Key Phase floor to a repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle, including old-key retention and discard, without treating the existing first-update helper as full RFC 9001 support.
planned_changes:
- Preserve the bounded repeated-update control state that now exists above the first-successor floor for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003`: current-phase acknowledgment tracking and the three-PTO post-confirmation local-update cooldown.
- Preserve the bounded repeated local install floor that now derives and installs phase-2 material only after current-phase acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old first-update discard, and emits outbound Key Phase using key-phase parity.
- Preserve the bounded phase-3 local install floor that now derives and installs phase-3 material only after phase-2 acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old phase-1 discard.
- Preserve the bounded phase-4 topoff that now derives and installs local phase-4 material only after phase-3 acknowledgment, cooldown, and retained-old phase-2 discard, opens peer phase-4 packets after phase-3 confirmation, and sends ACKs with phase-4 send keys.
- Preserve the bounded phase-5 topoff that now derives and installs local phase-5 material only after phase-4 acknowledgment, cooldown, and retained-old phase-3 discard, opens peer phase-5 packets after phase-4 confirmation, and sends ACKs with phase-5 send keys.
- Preserve representative phases 6 through 24 property/fuzz coverage for the repeated local-update gate, peer next-key opening, old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity, and retained-previous cleanup shape without claiming a full unbounded epoch ledger.
- Preserve the bounded repeated lower-recovered receive-selection floor that now authenticates retained phase-1 packets after local phase-2 install, retained phase-2 packets after local phase-3 install, retained phase-3 packets after local phase-4 install, and retained phase-4 packets after local phase-5 install, rejects retained packets with stale Key Phase bits, and tracks the current packet-number floor per key phase.
- Preserve the phase-2, phase-3, and phase-4 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity now proven when a retained old-key ACK acknowledges a newer-key send.
- Preserve the timing-neutral invalid-Key-Phase classification now proven for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001` and the current timing-neutral no-close policy for apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates under `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003`.
- Preserve the helper-backed AEAD key lifecycle and policy floor now proven for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005`: per-key-set counters, confidentiality-limit stop-use, update-required classification, connection stop-use classification, and stateless-reset-only classification.
- Preserve the bounded runtime AEAD-limit wiring now added to 1-RTT send/open paths: first-update-before-excess-protection, repeated-update-before-excess-protection after the repeated-update gate opens, successor-protection discard, and receive-side integrity-limit discard without close-datagram emission.
- Preserve bounded endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset emission after AEAD-limit terminal discard, including token availability, loop-prevention, and rate gates.
- Preserve bounded retained-route host stateless-reset response after AEAD-limit terminal discard, including token-linked route lookup, same-remote path matching, loop-prevention, and rate gates.
- Preserve the existing first-successor install path as a bounded prerequisite rather than widening receive-side repeated epoch selection in place.
- Preserve the bounded repeated old-key discard cleanup floor that now arms retained phase-1 cleanup from local and peer phase-2 packets, retained phase-2 cleanup from phase-3 packets, retained phase-3 cleanup from phase-4 packets, retained phase-4 cleanup from phase-5 packets, and representative retained-previous cleanup from later phases, then expires matching sender/recovery state without clearing current-phase acknowledgment/cooldown ownership.
- Keep broader stateless-reset response behavior blocked until that follow-on has explicit requirements and a proof plan.
- Keep TLS KeyUpdate prohibition, post-handshake ticket ingestion, 0-RTT, public API promises, and interop expansion out of this RFC 9001 lifecycle slice unless separately traced.
verification_plan: The executable slice must include positive tests for repeated local and peer updates, negative tests for duplicate/stale/tampered transitions, current-phase acknowledgment gating, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, AEAD-limit-driven update requests, endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset-only emission after terminal discard, automatic retained-route host reset response after terminal discard, and premature discard, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation, epoch transition, and reset-emission gate boundaries, and BenchmarkDotNet coverage for hot successor-derivation/install/open, AEAD lifecycle policy, and stateless-reset helper paths. The current first-update tests, S6P6 runtime wiring tests, QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks, QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks, QuicAeadKeyLifecycleBenchmarks, and QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks remain bounded evidence only.
completion_notes: Planned for the broader repeated lifecycle. The direct first-successor proof for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 is now executable at the requirement-home level, including retained old first-update 1-RTT packet-protection material before and after new-key packet authentication plus explicit retained cleanup. The first peer-initiated update proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004 for opening next-key packets only when they carry the next Key Phase bit, updating send keys before ACK emission, dropping apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates without close/datagram signaling under the current optional policy, and allowing KEY_UPDATE_ERROR when a retained old-key ACK packet acknowledges a tracked first-successor phase-1 send. The invalid Key Phase timing-neutral proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001 by dropping current/next key-phase mismatches without close/datagram signaling or successor installation. The first next-receive-key retention proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002 by retaining current and next receive material for apparent peer updates and clearing the retained next material once the first successor is installed. The first send-key ordering proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0002 for first-update ACK sends and locally built higher-packet-number packets. The first old/current next receive-selection proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 for rejecting old-key packets that violate the first successor packet-number floor, using retained old receive material when recovered packet numbers fall below the current phase floor, and using next receive material when recovered packet numbers rise above that floor. The repeated local-update proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003 by tracking acknowledgment of the current key phase, exposing a three-PTO post-confirmation eligibility gate, requiring retained-old first-update discard before phase-2 install, emitting outbound Key Phase by phase parity, allowing a bounded local phase-3 install after phase-2 acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard, allowing a bounded local phase-4 install after phase-3 acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard, and allowing a bounded local phase-5 install after phase-4 acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard. The repeated lower-recovered receive-selection proof now extends REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 by authenticating lower recovered phase-1 packets after local phase-2 install, lower recovered phase-2 packets after local phase-3 install, lower recovered phase-3 packets after local phase-4 install, and lower recovered phase-4 packets after local phase-5 install, rejecting retained packets with stale Key Phase bits, and isolating the current packet-number floor by key phase. The repeated peer successor proof now extends REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 by accepting peer phase-2, phase-4, and phase-5 packets only after current-phase confirmation and prior old-key discard, retaining successor receive material for apparent repeated peer updates, sending ACKs with the installed phase's keys, and preserving timing-neutral drops for unconfirmed consecutive peer updates. The phase-3 through phase-5 peer/update proof now extends REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 by opening bounded peer phase-3/phase-4/phase-5 packets after confirmation, applying KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity when retained old-key ACK packets acknowledge newer-key sends, and using next keys for phase-4 and phase-5 higher recovered packet numbers. The first retained-old discard proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 by arming a three-PTO retained-old discard timer after the first authenticated phase-1 packet and discarding the retained old read material together with matching sender and recovery state when that timer expires. The helper-backed AEAD key lifecycle, policy, and bounded runtime proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005 for per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, update-required classification, connection stop-use classification, stateless-reset-only classification, first AEAD-limit-triggered key update on send, bounded repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local update after the repeated-update gate opens, successor-protection discard, receive-side integrity-limit discard without close-datagram emission, endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset emission after AEAD-limit terminal discard, and automatic retained-route host stateless-reset response after AEAD-limit terminal discard. The repeated old-key discard cleanup proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 after local and peer phase-2 installs plus local phase-3, phase-4, and phase-5 installs by arming retained phase-1, phase-2, phase-3, or phase-4 cleanup, expiring it with matching sender/recovery state, and preserving current-phase acknowledgment/cooldown state for the current epoch. Remaining planned work is broader repeated epoch-ledger cleanup and stateless-reset response behavior outside the proven AEAD-limit retained-route floor; no general repeated 1-RTT key-update implementation is claimed by this artifact.

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Boundary
supplemental_sections.content: The current executable floor can prove the first 0-to-1 successor install, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, first current-phase acknowledgment tracking, the first three-PTO post-confirmation repeated local-update cooldown, bounded local phase-3 install after phase-2 acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard, bounded local phase-4 install after phase-3 acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard, bounded local phase-5 install after phase-4 acknowledgment/cooldown/old-key discard, timing-neutral invalid Key Phase drop classification, timing-neutral no-close handling for apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, the first retained-old discard timer after authenticating a new-key packet, first old-key discard with matching sender/recovery cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, bounded peer phase-3, phase-4, and phase-5 opening, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge first-successor phase-1 sends, phase-2, phase-3, and phase-4 old-key ACK KEY_UPDATE_ERROR parity for newer-key sends, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, retained phase-2, phase-3, and phase-4 lower recovered selection after repeated local install, phase-4 and phase-5 higher recovered next-key selection, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, helper-backed per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification, bounded runtime first-update/repeated-update-or-discard send/open wiring, bounded endpoint helper-backed stateless-reset emission after AEAD-limit terminal discard, bounded retained-route host stateless-reset response after AEAD-limit terminal discard, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets. Bounded local and peer phase-2 cycles after acknowledgment are now proven, including retained phase-1 old-key discard with sender/recovery cleanup; bounded local phase-3, phase-4, and phase-5 topoffs with retained phase-2/phase-3/phase-4 cleanup are also proven. Broader stateless-reset response behavior remains planned until the response matrix is implemented and proven.

supplemental_sections.heading: Traffic Secret Advancement Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Add a pending 1-RTT traffic-secret update carrier so derived successor secrets are zeroed unless the matching bridge-state install commits.
- Replace stored application traffic secrets with the updated `quic ku` secrets when the first local or peer-initiated update commits.
- Allow helper derivation of the next successor material from the advanced secrets for bounded local, peer phase-2, bounded phase-3, bounded phase-4, bounded phase-5, representative later-phase checks, and repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local install paths while keeping broader cleanup blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Local Install Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Install a repeated local update only after current-phase acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old first-update discard.
- Retain the displaced phase-1 open/protect material when phase 2 becomes current.
- Emit outbound Key Phase using key-phase parity so phase 2 writes bit 0.
- Keep broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Lower-Recovered Receive Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Authenticate lower recovered phase-1 packets with retained previous material after bounded local phase-2 install.
- Authenticate lower recovered phase-2 packets with retained previous material after bounded local phase-3 install.
- Authenticate lower recovered phase-3 packets with retained previous material after bounded local phase-4 install.
- Authenticate lower recovered phase-4 packets with retained previous material after bounded local phase-5 install.
- Reject retained packets that carry stale Key Phase bits for the immediately previous phase.
- Track the current packet-number floor by key-phase counter so earlier observations do not poison later repeated ordering.
- Keep broader epoch-ledger widening blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Peer Second-Successor Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Retain second-successor receive material after current-phase confirmation and prior retained-old discard.
- Authenticate peer phase-2 packets with Key Phase bit 0 and install matching phase-2 open/protect material.
- Send ACKs for repeated peer-updated packets with phase-2 send keys.
- Preserve timing-neutral drops for unconfirmed consecutive peer updates.
- Keep broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Old-Key Discard Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Arm retained phase-1 cleanup from authenticated local and peer phase-2 packets.
- Arm retained phase-2 cleanup from authenticated phase-3 packets after bounded local phase-3 install.
- Arm retained phase-3 cleanup from authenticated phase-4 packets after the bounded phase-4 topoff.
- Arm retained phase-4 cleanup from authenticated phase-5 packets after the bounded phase-5 topoff.
- Exercise representative retained-previous cleanup across phases 6 through 24.
- Expire retained old read material together with matching sender/recovery state.
- Keep current send, recovery, packet-protection, acknowledgment, and cooldown state intact after retained old cleanup.
- Keep a real unbounded epoch-ledger cleanup model blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded AEAD-Limit Stateless-Reset Endpoint Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Preserve stateless-reset token and path binding state after AEAD-limit terminal discard.
- Emit a stateless reset through `TryCreateStatelessResetDatagram` only when the caller supplies a retained handle/sequence with a registered token.
- Emit automatic retained-route stateless resets from the connected endpoint host and listener host only when a later packet resolves to a token-linked retained route from the same remote address and port.
- Keep loop-prevention and rate-limit gates active after terminal discard.
- Keep broader stateless-reset response behavior blocked until the response matrix is traced.

supplemental_sections.heading: Next Planned Scope
supplemental_sections.content:
- Keep broader stateless-reset response behavior outside the bounded AEAD-limit retained-route floor blocked until packet-to-token lookup, response eligibility, loop prevention, and rate gating are traced.
- Continue repeated key-update work with the dedicated `9001-06-repeated-epoch-ledger-ownership` follow-on beyond the representative later-phase proof.
